task_completion.description: Create the required layers with correct colors and linetypes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Layer List panel in LibreCAD shows 'MECHANISM' (Blue), 'CONSTRUCTION' (Gray/Custom, Dot linetype), and 'DIMENSIONS' (Red).

task_completion.description: Draw the Ground link from the origin horizontally.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A horizontal line segment (likely blue) starting at the origin (0,0) and extending to the right along the X-axis.

task_completion.description: Draw the Crank link at a 60-degree angle from the origin.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A line segment (likely blue) starting at the origin (0,0) and angled upwards and to the right at approximately 60 degrees.

task_completion.description: Draw construction geometry to find the intersection for the Coupler and Rocker links.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Gray, dotted circles or arcs visible on the canvas, centered at the ends of the Crank and Ground links, intersecting to form the mechanism's third point.

task_completion.description: Draw the Coupler and Rocker links to complete the four-bar mechanism.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Two connected line segments (likely blue) that connect the free end of the Crank link to the free end of the Ground link, completing a closed quadrilateral shape.

task_completion.description: Add an angular dimension for the Rocker link relative to the ground.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A red angular dimension arc with text, placed between the Rocker link and the Ground link (or the horizontal axis).

task_completion.description: Save the drawing to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreCAD window title bar or a save dialog shows the filename 'linkage_study.dxf' in the /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/ directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreCAD graphical user interface to create the drawing.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the LibreCAD application open with visible drawing tools, command line inputs, and canvas interactions.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a Python script or terminal command to generate the DXF file directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal windows do not show the execution of scripts (e.g., using ezdxf) to bypass manual drawing in LibreCAD.

integrity.description: The agent actually constructed the geometry rather than just opening a pre-existing solution file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Progressive screenshots show the step-by-step creation of lines, layers, and circles, rather than the final drawing appearing instantly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The linkage parameters are: Ground=200mm (from 0,0 to 200,0), Crank=60mm at 60 degrees, Coupler=250mm, Rocker=150mm. The drawing must use three layers: 'MECHANISM' (Blue), 'CONSTRUCTION' (Gray, Dot linetype), and 'DIMENSIONS' (Red). The final file must be saved as 'linkage_study.dxf' in /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/.
